definition: An optimization algorithm used to minimize the cost function in various machine learning algorithms.
When a model learns the training data too well, including the noise, causing it to perform poorly on unseen data.
The probability of obtaining test results at least as extreme as the results actually observed, under the assumption that the null hypothesis is correct.
When a model is too simple to capture the underlying structure of the data, resulting in poor performance on both training and unseen data.
A technique used to prevent overfitting by adding a penalty term to the loss function (e.g., L1 Lasso, L2 Ridge).
A resampling procedure used to evaluate machine learning models on a limited data sample, typically to prevent overfitting.
A method of evaluating how well an algorithm models the given data. Lower loss indicates a better model.
A hyperparameter that controls how much to change the model in response to the estimated error each time the model weights are updated.
A configuration whose value is set before the learning process begins, unlike parameters which are derived during training.
One complete pass of the training dataset through the machine learning algorithm.
The number of training examples utilized in one iteration before updating the internal model parameters.
A type of machine learning where the model is trained on a labeled dataset.
A type of machine learning that looks for previously undetected patterns in a dataset with no pre-existing labels.
A supervised learning approach where the target variable is discrete (e.g., spam vs. not spam).
A supervised learning approach where the target variable is continuous (e.g., predicting house prices).
Combining multiple machine learning models to create a more robust and accurate model.
An ensemble learning method that constructs a multitude of decision trees at training time.
A computational model inspired by the way biological neural networks in the human brain process information.
A subset of machine learning based on artificial neural networks with multiple layers (deep architectures).
A field of AI focused on the interaction between computers and humans through natural language.
A type of neural network trained on vast amounts of text data to generate human-like language.
The tension between the error introduced by the bias and the variance, crucial for understanding model performance.
The process of reducing the number of random variables under consideration, by obtaining a set of principal variables.
The process of using domain knowledge to extract features from raw data via data mining techniques.
